Category: Finance & Professional Services: Banking, Insurance, Accounting, Consulting, Legal, Real Estate
Who is this for: Finance managers, accounts payable clerks, treasury staff, banking officers, loan officers, financial advisors, compliance officers, accounting staff, payment processors, finance assistants, branch managers
Team or Dept this is for: Finance, Accounting, Treasury, Banking Operations, Compliance, Risk Management
Description: Impersonation threats in approvals and transactions.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with everyday financial processes such as approving transactions, verifying identities, or authorising payments is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key characteristics of deepfake impersonation threats and explain how they are used to manipulate financial authorisations and transactions. You will also be able to apply practical verification strategies to recognise suspicious activity and protect your organisation from fraudulent approvals.
